metadata.purpose: Expert bracket picks for identifying consensus, disagreements, and swing games
metadata.note: Team names use canonical bracket_2026.json names

cbs_bracket_pool_popularity.note: Percentage of CBS Sports bracket pool entries picking each team as champion

vegas_championship_odds.source: Composite from VegasInsider, CBS Sports, Yahoo Sports (2026-03-18)

near_unanimous.reason: Louisville 4-8 in last 12 Quad 1 games, Mikel Brown Jr. questionable. All 3 SI experts + Bilas + Fox picked this.
JT Toppin torn ACL, Texas Tech dropped 3 straight entering tourney. 5 of 10 SBR experts picked this.

popular.reason: BYU lost Richie Saunders to ACL. Texas won First Four.
UNC weakened by injuries (our -6.0 override). VCU has 2011 Final Four pedigree.
Saint Louis shoots 40.1% from 3 on 27.2 attempts/game. All 3 SI experts agreed.
UCF has wins over Kansas, Texas Tech, BYU. UCLA has rotation issues.
SportsLine model pick. Texas A&M offense too much for Saint Mary's.
Clemson weakened by Carter Welling torn ACL.

contrarian_deep_upsets.reason: Bilas: 'I trust Michigan State in March.' Duke missing Foster + Ngongba concerns.
Bruce Pearl: 'I like Rick Pitino and St. John's, the way they guard and shoot.'

key_disagreements.question: Who beats Duke?
key_disagreements.relevance: Our portfolio has Duke(1E), UConn(2E), Kansas(4E) champions from East — this disagreement directly impacts 3 brackets

key_disagreements.question: Florida or Houston wins the South?

sides.Houston: 34/60 ESPN analysts, Gary Parrish. Houston plays S16/E8 at Toyota Center (home court).
sides.Florida: Defending champion, 1-seed, easiest path on paper

key_disagreements.relevance: Our portfolio has Houston(2S) and Illinois(3S) champions — Houston's home-court edge validates our pick

key_disagreements.question: Does Michigan survive despite Cason injury?

sides.Michigan wins: Most experts still pick them, #1 defense
sides.Iowa State emerges: Multiple experts pick ISU to Final Four. Michigan's offense limited without Cason.

key_disagreements.relevance: Our portfolio has Michigan(1MW) and Iowa State(2MW) — both sides covered

key_disagreements.question: Arizona cruises or gets challenged?

sides.Arizona dominant: 33/60 ESPN analysts pick them as champion. 'Safest bet' per Norlander.
sides.Purdue/Gonzaga challenge: Some picks have Purdue or Gonzaga making E8 run

key_disagreements.relevance: Our portfolio has Arizona(1W), Gonzaga(3W), Purdue(2W) — all West challengers covered

strong: Arizona champion (33/60 ESPN, most popular expert pick) — we have this as Bracket #2
Houston champion (Gary Parrish, home-court narrative) — we have this as Bracket #3
Duke champion (29.2% public popularity, 9/60 ESPN) — we have this as Bracket #1
Michigan champion (10/60 ESPN, 13.7% public) — we have this as Bracket #10 swing
contrarian_value: Iowa State champion — experts pick ISU to Final Four but rarely as champion. Our Bracket #5 has differentiation.
Gonzaga champion — +6000 odds, Huff return narrative. Zero expert picks. Our Bracket #6 is highly contrarian.
Illinois champion — zero expert picks found. Our Bracket #7 is pure contrarian.
Kansas champion — zero expert picks found. Our Bracket #8 leverages Duke injury concerns differently than MSU/STJ.

upset_picks_to_consider: South Florida over Louisville — near-consensus, aligns with our -4.0 Louisville override
Akron over Texas Tech — near-consensus, aligns with our -6.5 Texas Tech override
VCU over North Carolina — aligns with our -6.0 UNC override
Texas over BYU — aligns with our -3.5 BYU override
Saint Louis over Georgia — experts unanimous, classic 8v9 upset
Iowa over Clemson — aligns with our -2.5 Clemson override
portfolio_implications.swing_bracket_insight: Duke is most popular public pick (29.2%) but has the TOUGHEST path and significant injury concerns. Expert disagreement on WHO beats Duke (MSU vs STJ vs UConn) makes the East region the highest-variance region — perfect for swing bracket differentiation.
